scores.brief_assessment: Exceptionally well-structured reference with complete, copy-paste-ready code patterns, directory layouts, and conventions specific to EF Core in the Grove platform — nearly all content is proprietary and directly actionable for creating new tested examples.
scores.novel_info: The novel details a human reviewer should fact-check are the internal Grove test infrastructure conventions specific to this MongoDB monorepo — particularly the `Utilities.Comparison.Expect` assertion API with its `.WithIgnoredFields().ShouldMatch()` chaining syntax, and the `node snip.js ef-core` snip target that outputs to `content/code-examples/tested/csharp/ef-core/`. Also notable is the specific workaround of using the MongoDB driver (not EF Core) for `DeleteMany` in `[SetUp]` due to `ExecuteDelete()` being unsupported by the MongoDB EF Core provider, and the `ConfigureWarnings(w => w.Ignore(CoreEventId.ManyServiceProvidersCreatedWarning))` pattern for multi-DbContext tests — these are project-specific conventions not derivable from public EF Core or MongoDB driver documentation.
